stories.headline: Gaza sports community pledges to continue legacy of killed goalkeeper
stories.summary: Slain seeking cooking gas, Gaza goalkeeper Saleem al-Ashqar joins more than 1,000 Palestinian athletes killed in war.

stories.headline: As Ukraine War Escalates, Witkoff and Kushner Are Focused on Iran
stories.summary: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are pivotal players at a moment when the posts of U.S. ambassador to Moscow and Kyiv are both vacant.

stories.headline: UN sounds ‘red alert’ over human rights catastrophe in Sudan’s el-Obeid
stories.summary: UN human rights chief Volker Turk issued the warning over growing fears of an imminent RSF assault.

stories.headline: Ukraine to call on NATO partners to deliver air defence after brutal Russian bombardment
stories.summary: Ukraine is calling on its partners to deliver air defence systems to Ukraine after a Russian aerial attack killed at least 30 people on 2 July. With NATO allies preparing to meet in Ankara, Kyiv is urging them not to delay what they have already promised.

stories.headline: ‘Ridiculous’ for US to maintain current Nato support, Trump warns ahead of alliance summit
stories.summary: President says Washington’s relationship with Nato is ‘not reciprocal’ and ‘they were not there for us’ in Iran warDonald Trump has said it is “ridiculous” for the US to continue its “one sided” relationship with Nato, less than a week before a summit of the military alliance in Ankara.Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform that “They were not there for us!!!” and Washington’s relationship with Nato “is not reciprocal”. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

stories.headline: At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
stories.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

stories.headline: Russia’s advance collapses in Ukraine, ‘40,000’ troops killed in June
stories.summary: Ukraine's long-range strikes hit targets in Russia, where anxiety is rising.

stories.headline: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
stories.summary: Pakistan’s President Asif Ali Zardari expressed sorrow over the crash, offered condolences to the families of those killed and wished the injured a speedy recovery.

stories.headline: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
stories.summary: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

stories.headline: Dissident Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies aged 70
stories.summary: Lam Wing-kee, who defied China by selling material critical of Beijing, passed away after suffering from lung cancer.

stories.headline: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.headline: Monaco bomb suspect is a woman from Ukraine
stories.summary: Prosecutors issue international arrest warrant for woman believed to have fled to Germany

stories.headline: Fed and ECB Seen Diverging in French Snapshot of War Aftermath
stories.summary: The Federal Reserve will still need to hike interest rates this year despite Thursday’s numbers pointing to labor-market weakness, according to two French chief economists.

stories.headline: Pilot Was Bitten by Bat After Swarm Invaded Hotel Room, Lawsuit Says
stories.summary: A pilot, who was treated for possible rabies exposure, sued Marriott International, claiming his Denver hotel failed to offer him a new room after he woke up to a swarm of bats last year.

stories.headline: At the L.A. Warehouse Fire, First It Was the Smoke. Now It’s the Smell.
stories.summary: Rotting food — millions of pounds of it — remains in an L.A. cold-storage facility damaged in a fire. Residents say the cleanup hasn’t been fast enough.

stories.headline: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. stories.headline: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems

stories.headline: Germany’s Merz defends NATO spending after Trump calls it ‘ridiculous’
stories.summary: Back and forth over defence spending comes as NATO leaders set to meet in Ankara next week.

stories.headline: Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee, once detained by China, dies in Taipei
'Security of the Baltics is our security': Merz meets Baltic leaders before NATO summit
stories.summary: Days before the NATO summit in Ankara, Germany’s chancellor and Baltic leaders in Berlin pledged stronger deterrence against Russia and more aid for Ukraine.

stories.headline: NATO to reaffirm iron-clad commitment to Article 5 at Ankara summit
stories.summary: As NATO leaders prepare for their summit in Ankara next week, a document seen by Euronews says all 32 members – including the US – are still committed to the alliance's principle of collective defence.

stories.headline: Zuma showing South Africa 'middle finger' by meeting Gupta brother - minister
stories.summary: Former President Zuma's trip to India reignites anger over a decade-old corruption scandal.

stories.headline: John Roberts, the US chief justice playing the long game
stories.summary: Under him the Supreme Court has defied Trump, while at the same time expanding the scope of presidential power

stories.headline: How crowds become stupider
stories.summary: Prediction markets suffer when we all think the same way

stories.headline: Trump will oppose heavy US AI regulation, says outgoing tech adviser
stories.summary: Sriram Krishnan tells the FT the president is against a centralised regulator as AI backlash grows

stories.headline: ECB’s Lagarde Will Attend Next Week’s Ecofin Instead of Vujcic
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will be attending next week’s meeting of European Union finance ministers in Brussels instead of her vice president.

stories.headline: European Stocks at Record, Book Fourth Straight Week of Gains
stories.summary: European stocks advanced on Friday, closing at a record high and recording a fourth straight week of gains as investors remained optimistic that the Federal Reserve will hold off on rate hikes for now.

stories.headline: Argentina Pushes $6 Billion in Repo Maturities Past Election
stories.summary: Argentina’s central bank extended maturities worth $6 billion on its repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 presidential election.

stories.headline: Yen Strengthens as Traders Brace for Holiday Intervention Risk
stories.summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.

stories.headline: Canadian Stocks Enjoy Broad Gains After New Pipeline Proposal
stories.summary: Canada’s stocks benchmark climbed Friday morning after Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a new pipeline project to the country’s West Coast.

stories.headline: OPEC Output Surged in June as Hormuz Flows Jumped, Survey Shows
stories.summary: OPEC’s crude oil production surged last month as Persian Gulf members restored exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid a peace accord between the US and Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

stories.headline: The AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals
stories.summary: At a time when markets are growing uneasy over whether the enormous sums being poured into artificial intelligence will ever pay off, the prices the sector commands for each unit of usage are drifting lower.

stories.headline: Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
stories.summary: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

stories.headline: Carlyle Hires Banks for $400 Million India IPO of RCM Business
stories.summary: Carlyle Group Inc. has hired JM Financial Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to advise on a potential initial public offering of its India-based healthcare revenue cycle management business, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Pubs to Get Short-Term World Cup Boost
stories.summary: UK pubs will be allowed to stay open for Monday’s early morning match, but the overall benefit of the World Cup will likely be short-lived. Bloomberg's Sam Unsed discusses on "The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: An AIDS-free generation is within reach, but not guaranteed
stories.summary: New tools and community-led care can save lives, but only if governments sustain the global HIV response.

stories.headline: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats

stories.summary: Law comes into effect that critics fear will further erode rights of Uyghurs and Tibetans, as well as allow Beijing to pursue dissidents abroadA new ethnic unity law has come into effect in China despite warnings from Taiwan, the United Nations and rights groups that it could threaten freedoms, especially for minorities.The Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress aims to forge a “shared” national identity among ethnic groups, for example by st

stories.headline: Killers of British couple in South Africa sentenced to life
stories.summary: The pair had been collecting rare plants and seeds before their bodies were found days later in a river.

stories.headline: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.headline: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool
stories.summary: US authorities charged him with property destruction after renovations championed by President Donald Trump appeared to go awry.

stories.headline: South Africa and Ghana in diplomatic row over alleged killing of migrant
stories.summary: South Africa denies claims that a Ghanaian citizen was killed in anti-migrant protests in Cape Town.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: Oil Is Little Changed as Persian Gulf Flows Near Pre-War Levels
stories.summary: Oil prices were little changed in thin trading as the recovery of Persian Gulf flows fomented a new surplus in key parts of the global market.

stories.headline: Louisiana Attorney General Charged With Bullying New Orleans Officials
stories.summary: Liz Murrill, a Republican, was indicted on charges of malfeasance and intimidation after sending warning letters to the city’s Democratic leaders over actions they took.

stories.headline: David Hearn, Olympian Canoeist, Is Indicted After Arrest at Trump’s Reflecting Pool
stories.summary: David Hearn, a former canoeist, is accused of vandalizing the algae-challenged pool, which President Trump has said he would repair and beautify with fresh sealant. But the algae returned and the paint peeled.

stories.headline: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and NigeriaFloods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan.There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. stories.headline: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
stories.summary: The 19-year-old dual American-Estonian national has been extradited to the US, where he is facing federal charges.

stories.headline: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: European Stocks Rally to Cap Best Week Since May: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks rose as the latest round of jitters about the artificial-intelligence trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark rising to an all-time high. The dollar touched a two-week low while gold extended gains.

stories.headline: Sudan’s RSF committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, Amnesty says
stories.summary: Report accuses paramilitary force of crimes including ethnic cleansing in systemic campaign against civiliansThe Sudanese paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during its campaign to capture El Fasher, Amnesty International has alleged.Many of the crimes, including murder, torture, rape, enslavement and sexual slavery, were carried out as part of a widespread and systematic attack against civilia

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
stories.summary: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

stories.headline: Greek politician's mother dies of wounds after arson attack
stories.summary: Three separate attacks involving petrol bombs and other improvised devices took place in Thessaloniki in the early hours of Wednesday.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.

stories.headline: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
stories.summary: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.headline: Big oil’s secretive trading arms are having an extraordinary year
stories.summary: Meet the corporate gamblers who never waste a good energy crisis

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
stories.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
stories.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match
stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.
